segments.text: The city has a perfect grid, but its most important government buildings completely ignore it. There's a brilliant reason why. This is for all the major buildings in the city. They
ignore the city plan to follow something more important, the sun. The visionary architect behind these buildings tilted them towards true north. This lets the
north facing windows catch gentle indirect light. To block the harsh southern sun, he used one of his signature ideas, massive concrete
sunbreakers that act like a permanent visor for the building. Results. These rule-breaking government buildings use 30% less electricity. It's a simple,
brilliant trick from the 1950s. Don't fight the climate. Work with it. A lesson in sustainable design that's more relevant than ever. If you want to know more such amazing design concepts,
